response.justification: While the candidate meets the education and experience baseline with 19 years of software development, they lack the specialized domain expertise required for this principal-level hardware architecture role. Their career is entirely software-focused with no evidence of SoC/IP modeling, GPU architecture analysis, or deep learning workload optimization—all core requirements. Current AI infrastructure work is a positive signal but appears to be tooling and frameworks, not accelerator performance optimization.
strengths: Exceeds minimum qualifications with bachelor's degree plus 19 years of development experience across diverse technical domains
Current role at re:cinq involves AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling, showing emerging engagement with AI systems
Demonstrated performance optimization skills from Dunnhumby role building low-latency, high-throughput systems handling millions of requests per second
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, chip architecture, or hardware description languages—fundamental to the role's core responsibilities
Missing required background in GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD systems, and deep learning workload analysis for AI accelerators
Entirely software-centric career with no hardware-software co-design experience or exposure to chip design tools and methodologies

key_factors.factor: SoC/IP modeling and hardware architecture expertise
GPU architecture and AI accelerator optimization experience
Senior IC level and proven engineering seniority
